description: Machine-readable GPU / hardware evidence status manifest (Bastion B16). Backs docs/BACKEND_ASSURANCE_MATRIX.md and is gated by ci/check_gpu_hardware_evidence.py (also audit_gate.py --gpu-hardware-evidence, principle G-16). It makes the GPU/hardware claim surface EXPLICIT and honest: which backend claims are backed by real-device evidence (owner-run; no GitHub GPU runners), which are host-side fallback correctness (committed, runs without a GPU), and which are owner-gated / documented residuals (ROCm real-device RR-003, schnorr_snark fallback RR-005, hardware power/EM RR-006). A fallback-correctness row may NOT be counted as native GPU performance evidence.

claim_type_legend.performance: Throughput/speed claim (must NOT be backed by a CPU fallback path).
claim_type_legend.fallback_correctness: Correctness established via the host/CPU reference path (no real device); must name a fallback_path and is never native-performance evidence.
claim_type_legend.out_of_scope: Explicitly out of the claimed assurance surface.

severity_legend.blocking: Cheap, committed evidence that must be current; missing evidence_path or stale last_verified fails the gate.
severity_legend.warning: Committed host-side evidence; staleness/missing is advisory + pre-alerted.
severity_legend.owner_gated: Real-device / host-only evidence run by the owner (no GitHub GPU runners); explicit and never counted as current evidence on push.
severity_legend.documented_residual: Accepted/deferred residual; must resolve to a docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md RR-id.

rows.operation: batch verify / public-data correctness (ecdsa/schnorr verify, bip352 scan)
rows.notes: CUDA public-data batch verify. Committed correctness = GPU-vs-CPU host-side differential + host-API tests (run WITHOUT a real GPU on GitHub runners). Real-device kernel + performance evidence is owner-run (docs/GPU_BACKEND_EVIDENCE.json, .github/workflows/gpu-selfhosted.yml); not native-performance evidence.
OpenCL public-data batch verify. Correctness via the committed backend-parity matrix + CPU-reference path; real-device evidence owner-run. Not native-performance evidence.
Metal public-data batch verify. Correctness via host-API + CPU-reference path (no Apple GPU on GitHub runners); real-device evidence owner-run. Not native-performance evidence.

schnorr_snark_witness_batch deterministic host/CPU fallback is byte-identical to the C ABI on all backends (correctness parity closed). Native CUDA/OpenCL/Metal kernels exist (2026-04-24); this row is fallback CORRECTNESS only and is explicitly NOT native-throughput evidence (RR-005).

rows.replay_command: owner: run the GPU test suite on an AMD ROCm/HIP device and archive docs/GPU_BACKEND_EVIDENCE.json
rows.notes: ROCm/HIP is an early-development compatibility path via the shared CUDA/HIP portability layer; AMD hardware-backed evidence is not part of the claimed surface. Owner-gated real-device lane; explicitly not current evidence. Cross-linked to RR-003.

rows.operation: hardware power / EM / fault-injection side channels
rows.replay_command: see docs/HARDWARE_SIDE_CHANNEL_METHODOLOGY.md (dudect-on-silicon / DPA workstation methodology to lift the deferral)
rows.notes: Power/EM/microarchitectural-injection and fault-injection side channels are explicitly out of scope (operating-environment auditors). The library claims only software-side-channel CT (CT-layer + CT tooling). Tracked as RR-006.

rows.operation: GPU context thread-safety / one-context-per-thread caller contract
rows.notes: DOCUMENTED CALLER CONTRACT, not a library-proven correctness test: THREAD_SAFETY.md states a ufsecp_gpu_ctx* must not be shared across threads without external synchronization. The library does NOT run a multi-thread context-race test; honoring one-context-per-thread (or adding external sync) is the caller's responsibility (out of the library's proven guarantee). The gpu_host_api_negative test exercises API-boundary guards (NULL/edge cases), not thread races, so it is not cited as validation here. Adversarially verified 2026-06-13 (B16 verification workflow flagged the prior 'correctness' label as an over-claim).
